Even though a Sign is afflicted by the unfavourable transits
therefrom of the benefics, yet no harm would come to the objects
governed by it, provided it is aspected by strong benefics. However,
the resulfwould be utterly bad if under the unfavourable transits of
benefics the Sign be aspected by strong malefics.
[The GocaraptdU or transit-affliction mentioned here refers to
the benefics occupying houses other than those mentioned in verses
9 and 10. These benefics may aspect the Sign, though they them-
selves may be posited in unfavourable houses. The result of this
aspect must do some good at least.]

Chapter XLII—Fluctuation of Prices
^fTsm^TFTimfrtTTcng qT'orm^t ^ n?n

By observing excessive rain, meteor, Rod, halo, eclipse, mock-
sun and such other portents on the new-Moon and full-Moon days
and also at the Sun's entrance into a new Sign of the Zodiac
every month, one should predict fluctuations in the prices of com-
modities.
